specification.topic-statement: The Anthropic streaming event processor dispatches SSE events to accumulate text deltas, thinking blocks, tool call arguments, and usage metadata into a streaming context, emitting normalized stream events to the caller at each significant boundary.

scope.in-scope: Per-event dispatch logic for all Anthropic SSE event types; model and usage extraction; content block type identification and initialization; sub-handler routing for delta types; tool state accumulation; event emission for start, text delta, thinking delta, tool call start, tool call delta, tool call done, done, and error; error type to category mapping for in-stream errors; and malformed JSON handling.
scope.boundary: How the accumulated streaming context (model, usage, tool state, thinking text, signature) is assembled into the final in-memory response structure is covered by the Anthropic response builder specification.
Translation of Anthropic stop_reason strings to the internal finish reason enumeration is covered by the finish reason mapping specification.
Splitting the raw HTTP body into individual event-type and data-string pairs is covered by the SSE parser specification. This processor receives already-separated event name and data strings.
HTTP-level error handling for non-streaming Anthropic error responses is covered by the provider error categorization specification. This specification covers only in-stream error events.

field.name: model
field.note: populated on message_start; null until then

field.name: finish_reason
field.note: populated on message_delta; UNKNOWN until then

field.name: usage.total_tokens
field.note: computed on message_delta as input + output + thinking

field.name: current_block_index
field.note: index of the active content block; -1 between blocks

field.name: current_block_type
field.note: type of the active content block

field.name: current_tool_args
field.note: accumulated argument JSON fragments; null if no delta arrived

field.name: current_thinking_text
field.note: accumulated thinking deltas; null if no delta arrived

field.name: current_thinking_signature
field.note: last-received signature; null if no signature delta arrived

field.name: current_redacted_data
field.note: data field from redacted_thinking block start

behavior.ping-handling: Ping events are silently discarded without parsing or emitting any event. All other event types proceed to JSON parsing.
behavior.malformed-json-handling:
Before dispatching any event, the data string is parsed as JSON. If parsing fails (invalid JSON), an ERROR event is emitted immediately with category UNKNOWN and a fixed message indicating invalid JSON. If parsing succeeds but the root value is not a JSON object, an ERROR event is emitted with category UNKNOWN and a fixed message indicating the data is not a JSON object. In both cases no further processing of the event occurs.
Malformed or structurally invalid SSE data is always surfaced as an ERROR event. The processor does not silently skip events with unparseable data.
behavior.unknown-event-handling: Event type strings not recognized by the processor are silently ignored after successful JSON parsing. No error is emitted for unknown event types.
behavior.message-start-processing:
On receiving a message_start event, the processor extracts data from the nested "message" object:
- The "model" string is extracted and stored in the streaming context. If the message object or the model field is absent, the model remains null.
- The "input_tokens" integer is extracted from the nested "usage" object within the message and stored as the input token count. If the usage object or the field is absent, the count remains zero.
After extraction, a START event is emitted carrying the model string (which may be null if it was not present in the event).
behavior.content-block-start-processing:
On receiving a content_block_start event, the processor reads the "index" integer and the "content_block" object. If the content_block object is absent or not an object, or if the "type" string field is absent, the event is silently ignored.
Block type dispatch:
- "text": the current block type is set to text. No event is emitted.
- "thinking": the current block type is set to thinking. No event is emitted.
- "redacted_thinking": the current block type is set to redacted thinking. The "data" string field from the content_block object is extracted and stored in the streaming context. No event is emitted.
- "tool_use": the current block type is set to tool call. Any previously accumulated tool arguments buffer is freed and reset to null. The "id" and "name" string fields are extracted from the content_block object and stored. A TOOL_CALL_START event is emitted immediately, carrying the index and the id and name values as they stand at the time of emission (either or both may be null if the fields were absent in the event).
TOOL_CALL_START is emitted as soon as a tool_use block begins, regardless of whether both id and name were successfully extracted. The event may carry null id or null name if either field was missing from the content_block_start payload.
When a tool_use block starts, any tool argument buffer from a prior block is discarded. This allows sequential tool calls to accumulate independently, though only the most recent tool state is retained.
behavior.content-block-delta-processing:
On receiving a content_block_delta event, the processor reads the "index" integer (defaulting to 0 if absent) and the "delta" object. If the delta object is absent or not an object, or if the "type" string field within it is absent, the event is silently ignored.
Delta type dispatch:
- "text_delta": the "text" string from the delta object is extracted and a TEXT_DELTA event is emitted carrying the index and the text pointer. No accumulation into the streaming context occurs.
- "thinking_delta": the "thinking" string from the delta object is extracted and appended to the accumulated thinking text buffer. A THINKING_DELTA event is then emitted carrying the index and the delta string pointer.
- "signature_delta": the "signature" string from the delta object is extracted. The existing thinking signature value in the streaming context is freed and replaced with the new value. No event is emitted.
- "input_json_delta": the "partial_json" string from the delta object is extracted and appended to the accumulated tool arguments buffer. A TOOL_CALL_DELTA event is emitted carrying the index and the partial string pointer.
The text pointer carried in a TEXT_DELTA event is not copied — it aliases directly into the SSE event data buffer. The pointer is ephemeral and must not be retained by the caller beyond the callback invocation.
Signature deltas use replacement semantics. Each new signature_delta discards the previous value and stores only the incoming string. If multiple signature deltas arrive (which is unusual), only the last one is retained. This is in contrast to thinking text and tool arguments, which are appended across deltas.
Unlike text_delta — which is only emitted, not stored — thinking_delta content is both emitted as a THINKING_DELTA event and appended to the accumulated thinking text buffer for inclusion in the final response.
behavior.content-block-stop-processing:
On receiving a content_block_stop event, the processor reads the "index" integer (defaulting to 0 if absent). If the current block type is tool call, a TOOL_CALL_DONE event is emitted carrying the index. For all other block types (text, thinking, redacted thinking), no event is emitted.
After the conditional emission, the current block index is reset to -1 to indicate no active block.
After emitting TOOL_CALL_DONE, the accumulated tool state (id, name, arguments) is deliberately left intact in the streaming context. The response builder reads this state when assembling the final response. Clearing the state here would cause the tool call to be lost from the response.
behavior.message-delta-processing:
On receiving a message_delta event, the processor extracts data from two top-level fields:
- The nested "delta" object may contain a "stop_reason" string. If present, it is translated to an internal finish reason enumeration value and stored in the streaming context.
- The top-level "usage" object (not nested inside "delta") may contain "output_tokens" and "thinking_tokens" integers. If present, they are stored in the streaming context. After extraction, the total token count is computed as input_tokens + output_tokens + thinking_tokens and stored.
No event is emitted for message_delta events.
behavior.message-stop-processing: On receiving a message_stop event, the processor emits a DONE event carrying the accumulated finish reason and the complete usage record (input, output, thinking, and total token counts). The event data payload from message_stop itself is not used.
behavior.in-stream-error-processing:
On receiving an error event, the processor extracts the "error" object from the root. If the error object is absent or not an object, an ERROR event is emitted with category UNKNOWN and a generic message.
When the error object is present, the "type" and "message" string fields are extracted. The "message" value (defaulting to "Unknown error" if absent) is carried directly in the emitted ERROR event. The "type" value is mapped to an error category:
- "authentication_error" → AUTH - "rate_limit_error" → RATE_LIMIT - "invalid_request_error" → INVALID_ARG - "overloaded_error" → SERVER - Any other type string, or absent type → UNKNOWN
An ERROR event is emitted with the mapped category and extracted message.
In-stream error events carry their own type string and are categorized by that string directly. This differs from non-streaming Anthropic errors, which are categorized solely by HTTP status code with the error body used only for structural validation.
